name: André Previn
abstract: André George Previn (; born Andreas Ludwig Priwin; April 6, 1929 – February 28, 2019) was a German and American conductor, composer, and pianist. His career had three facets: Hollywood films, jazz, and classical music.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] What’s that, I just read a quote from Andre Previn... [INTERVIEWEE] Well in harmony and in theory it’s really much easier to write for four voices or five voices.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Capp
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Andre for almost eight years. I must have made about 25 albums with him... Red and I and Andre were the Andre Previn Trio for over eight years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Capp interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I did a… Andre Previn got me involved in this project with Itzhak Perlman... Andre also had Shelly Manne and Red Mitchell come in from California. So it was a, it was a great group
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: I certainly did. That was one of the best sessions I ever had.

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions André Previn only in the context of Leroy Vinnegar's hit with 'My Fair Lady' (which Previn conducted/arranged) and as a contrast to Shelly Manne's group, but does not describe a personal interaction or professional collaboration with Previn himself.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: He played a concert there with Andre Previn. I don’t remember who was playing the guitar, but the three of them, and then next night, he was at Jazzland just jamming with Fritz and a local drummer. So, I took my horn and played some too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
